hook.text: If you are getting endo surgery, ask for excision, not ablation. Here's why
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Urgency and caution, warning against a potentially ineffective procedure
hook.target_audience_raw: Patients scheduled for or considering endometriosis laparoscopy

main_claim.text: Endometriosis should be treated with excision surgery, not ablation, because ablation only removes the surface and leaves deeper disease hidden, making it ineffective.

supporting_explanation.summary: Ablation is described as 'skimming' — analogous to skimming the surface of a plastered wall while the underlying problem remains. Endometriosis implants are too deep for surface removal, and disease will hide and persist if only ablated.

funnel_rationale: The content targets patients who already know they need or are planning endometriosis surgery, educating them on choice of surgical technique (excision vs ablation) to improve outcomes, typical of middle-funnel decision-support content.

cta.wording: She runs her own virtual clinic - link in bio.
cta.requested_action_raw: Click the link in bio to access the speaker's virtual clinic
cta.value_exchange_raw: Access to specialist endometriosis care via virtual clinic

topic.primary_raw: Endometriosis surgery technique: excision vs ablation
secondary_raw: Laparoscopy for endometriosis; specialist care access

speaker.primary_raw: Liz Bruen, Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist (CNS)

format_raw: Expert talking head with on-screen text hook
